rows.paper_expectation: Task source / IoT layer, edge agents, and cloud are represented as a three-tier CEC topology.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: Document the deterministic topology abstraction; no new simulator topology is claimed.
rows.simulator_behavior: The runtime layer uses deterministic topology profiles and scenario fixtures to represent the source, edge, and cloud tiers.

rows.paper_expectation: Multiple edge agents plus one cloud node exist as active runtime participants.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: Treat the active policy set and scenario contexts as the evidence boundary, not as a full distributed runtime simulator.
rows.simulator_behavior: The runtime policy set and scenario contexts model multiple edge agents plus a cloud destination.

rows.paper_expectation: Horizontal EA-to-EA destinations must be legal only when topology permits them.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: The legality model is documented through candidate/action legality checks.
rows.simulator_behavior: The policy layer distinguishes legal horizontal destinations from illegal attempts and rejects invalid horizontal choices.

rows.paper_expectation: Vertical offload reaches the cloud path and uses cloud-side execution capacity.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: The cloud path is evidenced by scenario traces, not by a separate cloud simulator service.
rows.simulator_behavior: The runtime scenarios include cloud-vertical fallback and vertical offload decisions with cloud destinations.

rows.paper_expectation: Tasks carry ID, data size, CPU demand/processing density, and deadline/timeout semantics.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: The task model is reconstructed through deterministic scenario fixtures and runtime rows.
rows.simulator_behavior: TaskBlueprint and scenario rows retain task_id, workload, deadline pressure, and arrival/completion timing fields.

rows.paper_expectation: Workload arrivals are represented over time or documented as deterministic approximations.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: Document the deterministic workload approximation; do not claim stochastic arrival fidelity.
rows.simulator_behavior: Scenario definitions encode workload and deadline-pressure profiles deterministically with seeds.

rows.paper_expectation: Local/private queue behavior contributes waiting and completion time before local execution finishes.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: This is an evidence-layer queue approximation, not a claim that the full FIFO queue simulator exists.
rows.simulator_behavior: Queue-length observations and delay traces are captured in the runtime rows, but the queue is represented through deterministic evidence rather than a standalone queue engine.

rows.paper_expectation: Offloaded tasks use offloading queues and transmission-time evidence before destination execution.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: The evidence is candidate-based, not a full queue-simulation replay.
rows.simulator_behavior: Horizontal and vertical candidate traces expose delay components and queue-length snapshots for offloaded actions.

rows.paper_expectation: Public/cloud queues receive vertically offloaded tasks and expose cloud-side waiting/execution behavior.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: The cloud queue is an audit abstraction and not a dedicated runtime queue class.
rows.simulator_behavior: The cloud path appears in the runtime outcomes and scenario traces; public/cloud queue behavior is documented through output timing rather than a separate queue model.

rows.paper_expectation: Local execution delay contributes to completion time.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: Local delay is evidenced in runtime rows, not by a standalone physical simulator.
rows.simulator_behavior: Local actions surface delay values and completion times in raw rows and aggregate metrics.

rows.paper_expectation: Horizontal offload delay includes transmission delay between edge agents.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: No additional repair required for the evidence layer.
rows.simulator_behavior: MLEO candidate traces expose horizontal transmission_delay and total_delay components.

rows.paper_expectation: Vertical offload delay includes EA-to-cloud transmission delay.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: No additional repair required for the evidence layer.
rows.simulator_behavior: Vertical candidates in the MLEO and policy traces expose transmission_delay and total_delay components.

rows.paper_expectation: Remote/cloud execution delay contributes to total delay for vertical offload.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: The remote compute term is evidence-backed, not a separate cloud runtime service.
rows.simulator_behavior: Cloud-bearing candidates carry compute_delay and total_delay evidence in the MLEO trace and runtime rows.

rows.paper_expectation: Waiting time and completion time are explicit parts of task latency accounting.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: Completion timing is represented in the audit layer, not as a full event simulator.
rows.simulator_behavior: Runtime rows capture arrival_time, completion_time, delay, and queue_length_observations.

rows.paper_expectation: Timeout, drop, and unavailability are reflected in output outcomes.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: The audit tracks these outcomes explicitly; it does not claim a full event-level simulator.
rows.simulator_behavior: Runtime rows and aggregate metrics include dropped_timeout, dropped_unavailable, and illegal_action_rejected flags.

rows.paper_expectation: Local, horizontal, and vertical actions exist as a hybrid offloading action space.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: No repair required; the hybrid action contract is already active.
rows.simulator_behavior: Active policies expose local, horizontal, and vertical families and the runtime policy set is the paper set.

rows.paper_expectation: Local-vs-offload and destination selection are distinct or are clearly documented when collapsed.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: Document the single-stage runtime approximation instead of pretending the training-stage boundary is reproduced.
rows.simulator_behavior: The runtime policy adapters operate as a documented single-stage approximation over legal candidate actions.

rows.paper_expectation: HOODIE remains the proposed method boundary and is not renamed or expanded into a thesis/DCQ method.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: No thesis/DCQ/custom-method claim is allowed.
rows.simulator_behavior: Feature 085/086 artifacts keep HOODIE as the proposed method boundary and do not introduce an alternate method claim.

rows.paper_expectation: Only RO, FLC, VO, HO, BCO, and MLEO are active baselines.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: No legacy active labels remain in the 086 outputs.
rows.simulator_behavior: The runtime evaluation policy registry exposes exactly the paper baselines plus HOODIE.

rows.paper_expectation: MLEO selects the legal candidate with minimum estimated total latency, not queue length only.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: No repair required for the evidence layer; keep the numeric proof.
rows.simulator_behavior: The candidate trace test proves MLEO chooses the minimum total_delay candidate when queue-length minimum is different.

rows.paper_expectation: Reward/cost equations remain inside the paper’s comparison boundary and are not overclaimed.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: Do not claim a trained end-to-end reward model; keep the boundary conservative.
rows.simulator_behavior: The runtime audit reports reward, delay, and drop outcomes with explicit claim-boundary text.

rows.paper_expectation: Metrics are classified so that paper-headline comparison and repository diagnostics are not conflated.
rows.required_fix_or_claim_boundary: Do not promote repository diagnostics to paper headline metrics.
rows.simulator_behavior: The metric readiness matrix classifies primary, secondary, and diagnostic metrics explicitly.
